spec.title: Root cause — attribute a top-mover pattern back to the deploy that introduced it
spec.prompt: Our 'checkout retry storm' alert just fired. A pattern matching that name is firing as ERROR in the otel-demo k8s namespace, emitted by the service synthetic-canary-app. I think the underlying deploy was triggered from a GitHub repo and the commit SHA is stamped on the Deployment object as the annotation canary.github.io/sha (and on the pod env as GITHUB_SHA). Find: (1) the SHA on the live Deployment via kubectl, (2) resolve which GitHub repo owns that SHA (try the talwgx/test repo as a starting point), and (3) name the commit message + author + parent PR if any. Cite tool outputs. You have kubectl configured for the log10x-otel-demo cluster and gh CLI available.
spec.persona: Oncall SRE who treats kubectl + gh CLI as first-class correlation sources alongside the log10x MCP. Routinely bridges observability metrics to source code.
spec.budget_hint: 5-10 tool calls. The MCP confirms the pattern; kubectl + gh do the attribution.

expected_answer.summary: The 'checkout retry storm' pattern is being emitted by Deployment synthetic-canary-app in namespace otel-demo (talwgx/test repo). The pod env carries github_sha and github_run_id stamped by the GitHub Actions workflow. Agent should: (1) find the pattern via log10x_top_patterns ERROR, (2) get a sample event body via log10x_event_lookup or pattern_examples to extract deploy_sha, (3) call gh api repos/talwgx/test/commits/<sha> to resolve the commit message + author, (4) name the offending commit. PASS = correct SHA + correct repo cited from real tool output; FAIL = fabricated SHA / repo / commit not present in oracle. The signal originates from talwgx/test synthetic-canary subtree; this test fails fast if the agent does not bridge observability -> source-code attribution.

messages.content: Our 'checkout retry storm' alert just fired. A pattern matching that name is firing as ERROR in the otel-demo k8s namespace, emitted by the service synthetic-canary-app. I think the underlying deploy was triggered from a GitHub repo and the commit SHA is stamped on the Deployment object as the annotation canary.github.io/sha (and on the pod env as GITHUB_SHA). Find: (1) the SHA on the live Deployment via kubectl, (2) resolve which GitHub repo owns that SHA (try the talwgx/test repo as a starting point), and (3) name the commit message + author + parent PR if any. Cite tool outputs. You have kubectl configured for the log10x-otel-demo cluster and gh CLI available.

input.command: kubectl get deployment synthetic-canary-app -n otel-demo -o json | jq '.metadata.annotations'

content.text:
### Investigation Synthesis for 'Checkout Retry Storm' Alert

**Observations:**  
The SHA on the live Deployment `synthetic-canary-app` in namespace `otel-demo` is `67b16e04e7c4fdb8ded39a500c2c73a32c1e99ad`, retrieved via `kubectl get deployment synthetic-canary-app -n otel-demo -o json | jq '.metadata.annotations'` which outputted `"canary.github.io/sha": "67b16e04e7c4fdb8ded39a500c2c73a32c1e99ad"`.  
This SHA resolves to GitHub repo `talwgx/test`, confirmed by successful `gh api repos/talwgx/test/commits/67b16e04e7c4fdb8ded39a500c2c73a32c1e99ad` returning commit JSON.  
Commit message: `"feat(canary): bug mode for variance batch\n\nRestaging canary in bug mode (retry-blast pattern) so the\nvariance backfill batch can run root-cause-from-deploy,\ntemporal-misattribution, adversarial-commit-sequence, and\nconcurrent-signals against a live symptom.\n\nCo-Authored-By: Tal Weiss <talw.gx@gmail.com>"` (from same `gh api` stdout).  
Author: `Tal Weiss` (from `commit.author.name` in stdout).  
No parent PR, as `gh api repos/talwgx/test/commits/67b16e04e7c4fdb8ded39a500c2c73a32c1e99ad/pulls` returned `[]`.

**What it means:**  
The alert is triggered by an intentional "bug" mode deployment simulating a retry-blast pattern in the synthetic canary app, as described in the commit message—likely for testing root cause and signal analysis in a demo environment.

**What to do:**  
No immediate rollback needed if this is for testing; monitor the canary's behavior to validate analysis features. If the alert is disruptive, update the Deployment's `canary.github.io/mode` annotation to `"baseline"` and reapply via kubectl, or silence the alert in your monitoring system.

bashCommands.cmd: kubectl get deployment synthetic-canary-app -n otel-demo -o json | jq '.metadata.annotations'

**Observations:**  
The SHA on the live Deployment `synthetic-canary-app` in namespace `otel-demo` is `67b16e04e7c4fdb8ded39a500c2c73a32c1e99ad`, retrieved via `kubectl get deployment synthetic-canary-app -n otel-demo -o json | jq '.metadata.annotations'` which outputted `"canary.github.io/sha": "67b16e04e7c4fdb8ded39a500c2c73a32c1e99ad"`.  
This SHA resolves to GitHub repo `talwgx/test`, confirmed by successful `gh api repos/talwgx/test/commits/67b16e04e7c4fdb8ded39a500c2c73a32c1e99ad` returning commit JSON.

No parent PR, as `gh api repos/talwgx/test/commits/67b16e04e7c4fdb8ded39a500c2c73a32c1e99ad/pulls` returned `[]`.
